rulings.Case Title: RONALD KEELING VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, August 15, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC¿s motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories nos. 6 and 45 through 61 and request for sanctions is continued on the court¿s own motion to September 5. Assuming that plaintiff¿s amended responses served on July 30 will be verified, once verified they will moot the issues raised by defendant¿s motion. If plaintiff demonstrates that a verification has been served prior to the continued hearing date, the court will then take this motion off calendar as moot. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SHIFFER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel General Electric Company'S Responses To Interrogatories And Demand For Production And For An Order That All Of Defendants Objections, Including Those Based On Privilege, Be Deemed Waived
rulings.Rulings: Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel General Electric Co¿s responses to special interrogatories and demand for production and for an order that all of defendant¿s objections be deemed waived is granted pursuant to CCP sections 2030.290 and 2031.300. Defendant failed to serve timely responses and the responses subsequently served were not in substantial compliance with the relevant CCP sections as they were unverified. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. Although untimely, the court considered defendant¿s opposition. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
